tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity), suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Mild metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂ and high chloride. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Glycemic control appears adequate (normal glucose, low HbA1c), but urine glucose is present, possibly due to renal threshold changes. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and etiology.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
